response.justification: Bogdan brings 15+ years of progressive full-stack engineering with deep TypeScript and Node.js experience that directly matches the primary stack. His current role at re:cinq explicitly covers AI agent infrastructure and MCP (Model Context Protocol) framework development, which precisely satisfies the AI agent development requirement. Testing culture is a career-long thread — he authored open-source testing libraries and ran TDD workshops. The main gaps are that NestJS is entirely absent from the resume despite being the core backend framework, React depth looks shallow compared to his heavier Ember.js history, and Kotlin is not mentioned. One additional flag: the MIT BS Computer Science dates (September 2009 – June 2012, Cambridge) overlap with simultaneous employment listed in Bucharest, Romania and Toronto, Canada during the same window — the education claim should be verified before advancing.
strengths: 15+ years of full-stack engineering with TypeScript and Node.js featured across multiple senior/staff-level roles
Current role includes hands-on AI agent infrastructure and MCP framework development — directly satisfies the AI agent requirement
Career-long TDD advocacy backed by concrete evidence: authored open-source testing libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial) and ran multi-day testing workshops
concerns: NestJS is entirely absent from the resume despite being the primary backend framework listed in the job stack
React experience appears limited to one role (Optilyz); the dominant frontend framework throughout the career is Ember.js, raising questions about React depth
MIT BS (2009–2012, Cambridge MA) dates overlap with concurrent employment listed in Bucharest and Toronto during the same period — requires verification

key_factors.factor: AI agent and MCP development at current role
TypeScript and Node.js depth across 15+ years
